programme.title: NWA Wrestle War 1989 The Music City Showdown
Sabu Mix Tape Raw VHS Rip
Aliens
programme.desc: Ripley, the sole survivor of the Nostromo's deadly encounter with the monstrous Alien, returns to Earth after drifting through space in hypersleep for 57 years. Although her story is initially met with skepticism, she agrees to accompany a team of Colonial Marines back to LV-426.

programme.title: Ballerina
programme.desc: An assassin trained in the traditions of the Ruska Roma organization sets out to seek revenge after her father's death.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: After arriving in India, Indiana Jones is asked by a desperate village to find a mystical stone. He agrees – and stumbles upon a secret cult plotting a terrible plan in the catacombs of an ancient palace.

programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: The wanted criminal Richard Bruno Riddick arrives on a planet called Helion Prime and finds himself up against an invading empire called the Necromongers, an army that plans to convert or kill all humans in the universe.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed vacation to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows
programme.desc: There is a new criminal mastermind at large (Professor Moriarty) and not only is he Holmes’ intellectual equal, but his capacity for evil and lack of conscience may give him an advantage over the detective.

programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York
programme.desc: Kevin is separated from his family again when he accidentally boards a flight to New York City during a Christmas trip to Miami. However he crosses paths with the same burglars, who now plan to rob a toy store on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Caddyshack
programme.desc: At an exclusive country club, an ambitious young caddy, Danny Noonan, eagerly pursues a caddy scholarship in hopes of attending college and, in turn, avoiding a job at the lumber yard. In order to succeed, he must first win the favour of the elitist Judge Smails, and then the caddy golf tournament which Smails sponsors.

programme.title: Napoleon Dynamite
programme.desc: Preston, Idaho's most curious resident, Napoleon Dynamite, lives with his grandma and his 32-year-old brother (who cruises chat rooms for ladies) and works to help his best friend, Pedro, snatch the Student Body President title from mean teen Summer Wheatley.

programme.title: Police Academy 6: City Under Siege
programme.desc: Our favourite police men are called together to deal with a gang who rob banks and jewelers. Using their various talents as well as their extraordinary luck, the crooks stand no chance against our men and women in blue.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds II: Nerds in Paradise
programme.desc: Get out your white socks! This time our suave college heroes hit the road for fun and fraternity in Florida. Another opportunity for brains to triumph over brawn. How can the girls possibly resist their geeky charms?

programme.title: Pee-wee's Big Adventure
programme.desc: The cartoonish and childish character Pee-wee Herman goes on a big adventure for the first time ever when his beloved shiny new bicycle is stolen by his nemesis Francis Buxton, a fellow man-child and neighborhood rich "kid." And he sets off on an obsessive cross-country journey, determined to recover it. Pee-wee's awkward and childish attempts to be cool and mature.

programme.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
programme.desc: After a family tragedy, three generations of the Deetz family return home to Winter River. Still haunted by Beetlejuice, Lydia's life is turned upside down when her teenage daughter, Astrid, accidentally opens the portal to the Afterlife.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds
programme.desc: At Adams College, the jocks rule the school from their house on high, the Alpha Beta fraternity. So when a group of socially-challenged misfits try to go Greek, they're instantly rejected by every house on campus. Deciding to start their own fraternity to protect their outcast brothers, the campus nerds soon find themselves in a battle royale as the Alpha Betas try to crush their new rivals.

programme.title: Back to School
programme.desc: Millionaire businessman Thornton Melon is upset when his son Jason announces that he is not sure about going to college. Thornton insists that college is the best thing he never had for himself, and to prove his point, he agrees to enroll in school along with his son. Thornton is a big hit on campus: always throwing the biggest parties, knowing all the right people, but is this the way to pass college?

programme.title: Austin Powers in Goldmember
programme.desc: The world's most shagadelic spy continues his fight against Dr. Evil. This time, the diabolical doctor and his clone, Mini-Me, team up with a new foe—'70s kingpin Goldmember. While pursuing the team of villains to stop them from world domination, Austin gets help from his dad and an old girlfriend.

programme.title: Happy Gilmore
programme.desc: A Hockey player wannabe finds out that he has the most powerful golf drive in history. He joins the P.G.A. tour to make some money to save grandma's house. The downside is that his hockey player mentality doesn't really go on the P.G.A. tour. Especially with the favorite to win the championship.i

programme.title: The Toy
programme.desc: On one of his bratty son Eric's annual visits, the plutocrat U.S. Bates takes him to his department store and offers him anything in it as a gift. Eric chooses a black janitor who has made him laugh with his antics. At first the man suffers many indignities as Eric's "toy," but gradually teaches the lonely boy what it is like to have and to be a friend.

programme.title: Austin Powers: The Spy Who Shagged Me
programme.desc: When diabolical genius Dr. Evil travels back in time to steal superspy Austin Powers's ‘mojo,’ Austin must return to the swingin' '60s himself - with the help of American agent, Felicity Shagwell - to stop the dastardly plan. Once there, Austin faces off against Dr. Evil's army of minions to try to save the world in his own unbelievably groovy way.

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail 1974
programme.desc: King Arthur, accompanied by his squire, recruits his Knights of the Round Table, including Sir Bedevere the Wise, Sir Lancelot the Brave, Sir Robin the Not-Quite-So-Brave-As-Sir-Lancelot and Sir Galahad the Pure. On the way, Arthur battles the Black Knight who, despite having had all his limbs chopped off, insists he can still fight. They reach Camelot, but Arthur decides not to enter, as "it is a silly place".

programme.title: Idiocracy
programme.desc: Corporal Joe Bauers, a decidedly average American, is selected for a top-secret hibernation program but is forgotten and left to awaken to a future so incredibly moronic that he's easily the most intelligent person alive.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A small town gradually becomes aware of a strange creature which picks off people one by one. But what is this creature, and where is it? At the same time, a seismologist is working in the area, she detects _tremors_. The creature lives underground, and can 'pop up' without warning. Trapped in their town, the town-folk have no escape.

programme.title: Tremors II: Aftershocks
programme.desc: Earl Bassett, now a washed-up ex-celebrity, is hired by a Mexican oil company to eradicate a Graboid epidemic that's killing more people each day. However, the humans aren't the only one with a new battle plan.

programme.title: Tremors 3: Back to Perfection
programme.desc: The now famous Burt Gummer returns to his hometown of Perfection, Nevada for the first time in years, but the deadliest Graboid evolution yet forces him to save the town he swore to protect.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Buck Cherry and Some Cheryl Crow Woodstock 99
Tom Petty and The Heartbreakers - Storytellers (1999)
Led Zeppelin Live Madison Square Garden
The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are private detectives hired to find a missing girl. The boys disguise as pie salesmen and end up wandering around a mad scientist's mansion, trying to find the girl. The boys confront a gorilla and various other bad guys, before rescuing the girl.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in a desert land where the stooges run a restaurant, the boys set out to recover the stolen Rootin Tootin diamond after they learn from the thieves that the Emir of Shmo has absconded with the contraband jewel. They journey to the stronghold of Shmo where they disguise as Santa Clauses and scare the ruler into giving them the diamond.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: To escape the police, the stooges pose as plumbers and are hired to fix a leak in a fancy mansion. The Stooges are would-be plumbers and mistake pipes filled with wires for water pipes. One of the most popular Stooge shorts. Dudly Dickersons battle in the kitchen is a classic, as well as his famous line: “This house is sho goin crazy!” Later remade with Shemp in “Vagabond Loafers” (1949) and “Scheming Schemers” (1956).

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are firemen at a station that still uses horses to pull the engines. A salesman who wants to sell the chief some modern equipment plants gun powder in one of the engines. The chiefs daughter catches him and after a chase both are knocked unconscious. When a fire starts, the stooges respond to the alarm, but don't realize its their firehouse that's burning! Somehow they manage to arrive in time to save the girl, and the villain gets his just desserts.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are facing eviction and decide to raise some money by becoming baby-sitters. Their first client is a women separated from her husband, who entrusts her son "Junior" to the boys' care. When The husband steals the baby, the stooges set out to find their missing charge and return him to his mother. The boys confront the husband and find Junior, and in the process the estranged couple is re-united.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges are lazy-bum husbands, inept salesmen, and misfit fugitives in a hospital.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges are exterminators who infest a mansion with pests during a fancy party.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc:
Svengoolie presents 'Billy the Kid vs Dracula'.
Dracula travels to the American West, intent on making a beautiful ranch owner his next victim. Her fiance, outlaw Billy the Kid, finds out about it and rushes to save her.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky is eager to practice for her driving test, and the rest of the family awaits the results of Roseanne's pregnancy test.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Lending money to pal Arnie comes easily for Dan, but he believes that borrowing from family only adds up to disaster.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne decides it's time to give Becky "the talk", unaware that it's Darlene who needs it more.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Despite her misgivings, Roseanne, working late as a coffee-shop waitress, leaves Dan in charge of the kids.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: After Police Officer Jackie gets slightly injured, Gary says he's tired of always worrying about her and wants her to quit her job as a Police Woman. Meanwhile, Becky and Darlene find something disturbing under D.J.'s bed that makes them believe he's a complete psycho.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky's folks pressure her to stop dating a boy.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne, mistaken for a guy in her lumberjack Halloween costume, plays along with some braggarts and learns about machismo.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne's PMS day and Dan's birthday are on a collision course.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: After Mr. Grace threatens Captain Peacock's career position, Peacock and the department staff decide to strike atop the roof of the department store. A decidedly bad idea, as it proves to be quite dangerous.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: While filling out an evaluation of Mr.Humphries work progress, the staff stumble upon a picture of Mr.Humphries mother. Then Mr.Grace recognizes her as a young girl he once left with child. To the shock of everyone, it turns out that Mr.Grace is Mr.Humphries father and consequently Mr.Humphries is the heir to Grace Brothers. But when Mrs.Humphries comes in to clear up the matter once and for all, everyone learns a shocking surprise!

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Toony opens up a pawn shop.
Featured cartoons: The Stupor Salesman (1948) Choose Your 'Weepins' (1935) Vitamin Pink (1966) Porky's Five & Ten (1938) A Bird in a Guilty Cage (1952)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Riley Greenleaf is a publisher and Alan Mallory, a successful writer, is his principal asset. But Alan Mallory has decided to change publishers. Riley threatens to kill Alan. Then he hires Eddie Kane, a Vietnam veteran, to murder Alan and to leave evidence that the killer is Riley himself, who has an excellent alibi. But a key will betray him.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude is convinced that her best friend, Vivian has the ""perfect marriage"" so she is completely stunned when Vivian announces plans for a divorce. This leads Maude to analyze her supposed ""perfect marriage"".

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude and Walter prepare for a dinner party at Cliff and Joanna Naylor's home. However, while they are getting ready, they discuss and bicker about Cliff's apparent crush on Maude and how she is flattered by him, that is until Walter breaks the news that Cliff is having an affair with someone else at the country club.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: One of Dorothy's students writes a prize-winning essay on what it means to be an American. After this, the Department of Immigration and Naturalization Services reveals that the boy is in the country illegally.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: While Dorothy and Rose are planning a hospital charity banquet, Blanche falls for Jake, the caterer hired for the event. She quickly learns he's more blue jeans than a black tie which prompts her to foolishly put a stop to their romance.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy's daughter, Kate is coming for a visit and has a big announcement. Dorothy is excited when she thinks the announcement is going to be that Kate is pregnant. However, when Kate arrives, her announcement is far from exciting. Meanwhile, Blanche and Rose stay up and watch an "I Love Lucy" marathon.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Sophia runs away to her old rest home. When the other girls try to get her, they find that it is no longer anywhere near as bad as she said it was.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred and Donna's on again off again relationship is back on and he invites her to dinner. Later, Fred gets a visit from a saleslady who peaks his interest and he blindly invites her to dinner, forgetting all about Donna. Thanks to Lamont's manipulations, he makes sure Fred has to juggle the dates around, keeping up appearances.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike accidently breaks Archie's Chair.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike asserts his independence by leaving Gloria behind while he joins his friends on a weekend skiing trip.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie is shocked when his coworker Stretch has died after cavorting with a young woman. Asked to give the eulogy, he enlists Mike's help, requesting Bible references. But the biggest shock comes at the funeral.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry has a hard time getting membership in the Friar's Club when the Flying Sandos Brothers take his jacket during a show. Meanwhile, Elaine suspects that a new co-worker (Rob Schneider) is faking a hearing disorder to get out of doing work.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Susan's friends are in town for the Broadway production of Joseph And The Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat. Kramer gets to wear the coat and is mistaken for a pimp in public when he accuses a local parking lot of running a prostitution ring out of his car.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne begins to feel the empty-nest syndrome when Jackie departs for police training, Becky dates a 17-year-old and Darlene goes to a co-ed party.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: A kiss is just a kiss, but it could mean trouble when the kisser is Dan's buddy Arnie, and the kissee is Roseanne.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: When Michael visits Dunder Mifflin corporate headquarters in New York on Valentine's Day, he and Jan are both in for a surprise. Meanwhile, back in Scranton, the office staff celebrates Valentine's Day grade school style.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Dwight must confront his fear of public speaking when he is named Dunder-Mifflin's Salesman of the Year. To prepare for a big convention where he is set to give a speech, Dwight turns to Michael to guide him in his quest to conquer the fear. Meanwhile, Pam gets wrapped up with wedding plans, but Jim has plans of a different sort: an escape route out of Dunder-Mifflin and Scranton, at least for long enough so that he doesn't have to witness Pam's wedding. While all of this is happening, the staffers battle it out over control of the thermostat.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.sub-title: Take Your Daughter to Work Day
programme.desc: A routine office day is changed when children come to Dunder Mifflin for "Take Your Daughter to Work Day". Michael is surprised when he strikes up a friendship with the five-year old daughter of his sworn enemy, Toby. Pam is desperate to befriend her colleagues' offspring and a misunderstanding puts Ryan under Stanley's thumb.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Lastest Gun in the West
programme.desc: While running away from a vicious dog, Bart meets Buck McCoy, a former Western film star. Bart begins hanging out with him and starts to idolize him. Bart wants to help McCoy stage a comeback, so he convinces all of the kids in town to become interested in the Wild West. McCoy appears on the Krusty the Clown Show, but the comeback flops when Buck begins drinking again and injures Krusty the Clown.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Old Man and the Key
programme.desc: Grampa falls in love with Zelda, a woman who has an interest in men who can drive. He decides to get his driver's license back, but is ignorant to Homer and Marge's concerns that she is only using him for his car.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer gets a notice from the library that he has a book of classic tales that is years overdue, he finds it on the shelf and reads three stories: The Odyssey (where Homer and his bar buddies try to get home after fighting the Trojans), Joan of Arc (where Lisa leads the French against the English with the help of God), and Hamlet (where Bart tries to kill Moe after Moe kills Homer in order to marry Marge).

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer gets the family's telephone service cut off for refusing to pay for calls made to Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, Lisa confesses that she was the one who called Rio after sponsoring an orphan who goes missing. She convinces the family to travel to Brazil to look for him. However, once there, they have no luck finding him, and Homer is kidnapped.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is prescribed medicinal marijuana after getting pecked in the eyes by a murder of crows. While his family and friends worry about the drug altering his personality, Homer becomes Mr. Burns's vice president after cracking up at Burns's antiquated jokes.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al opposes the proposed destruction of his old high school football field to build a new auto plant (initiated by Marcy and Kyoto Bank) by chaining himself to the goalpost. Meanwhile, Buck and Bud suffer from unusual side effects after Kelly the Verminator sprays them with a new pesticide called "Springtime in Baghdad."

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Jefferson reveals his secret past to Marcy when he tells her that he is a ex-CIA spy in a witness relocation program. Meanwhile, Al gets conflicting emotions when he is offered a $50,000 reward by a certain Walter Traugott, a shady thug who is looking for Jefferson. Elsewhere, Peggy has a toothache from a broken tooth, and Kelly and Bud dress up as bums at the mall to acquire more cash for themselves.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al shows his new temp Dexter around the shoe store, while Bud and Kelly queues outside for a rock concert, and Peggy, aiming to win a trip to Tahiti, tries to get Al to make a fool of himself over the radio.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al's carpool, consisting of him and three models, is chosen to promote a campaign to clean up Chicago.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When another complaint from a fat woman at the shoe store results in Al losing his parking space and being reprimanded not to insult anymore fat women, he, Jefferson and the rest of his mens club NO MA'AM seek out a legendary guru macho man to teach them how to battle political correctness. Meanwhile, Peggy, Kelly, and Bud hide a secret refrigerator full of food from Al.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al rushes to finish fixing the basement step before his favorite movie, "Hondo," comes on TV. But, in an attempt to get some new batteries for his flashlight, he finds himself locked inside the store. Meanwhile, Bud and Kelly attempt to celebrate Buck's birthday, much to Buck's dismay.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Using their tax refund money, Jefferson and Al bet heavily on a college football game that they are guaranteed to win. But that's before Trumaine's prized, not-so-bright quarterback Chad, who is being tutored by Bud, falls in love with Kelly, whom she pleasantly distracts from his training. Al must find a way to keep them apart at all costs after he finds out that other shady parties have also bet heavily on the game as well.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When the Bundy TV set blows its condenser, Al decides to try out for a place on a new sports trivia game show, hoping to win $10,000 for a new TV set as the first prize. But when he is denied because of his lack of personality, Al tries to transfer his knowledge of sports to Kelly to win the show for him. But for each fact that she takes in, another falls out.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred is tired of everyone walking all over him especially since he proclaims that he built his junk empire to what it is today, thus making him a king of an empire. Bubba suggests that Fred pay money to an agency to have them trace his roots. A couple weeks later Fred is in for a shock, he is a descendant from Jewish African kings.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Aspiring songwriter Fred invites B.B. King to dinner, but panics upon reading in the blues legend's biography that the singer has sworn to get even with the man who long ago stole his St. Louis sweetheart "E.W.", a woman matching Elizabeth's description.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont buys a porcelain figure for $15 from a silent movie star and after having it appraised, Lamont and Fred decide to take it to an auction to make even more money on it. Fred and Lamont attend the auction pretending to be buyers so that the price can be jacked up higher. However, things go awry to Lamont's dismay.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont buys Fred a new hat for his birthday and then tries to take him out on the town to celebrate. However, a visit to a fancy bar, a movie theater to see Fiddler on the Roof and dinner at a Chinese restaurant are far from a celebration for Fred.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Much to Sharon's dismay, Chris auditions for and gets the lead part in a local community theatre production of Andrew Todd Keller's musical Zoo Animals on Wheels.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris finds proof that makes him think he is adopted, so he sets off to find his natural parents, an Amish couple.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: In his never-ending quest for the good life, pushy mongrel Charlie attempts to ingratiate himself with his unwilling "master," Porky Pig.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: In a slick New York club for the rich and famous, Mr Humphrey Bogart orders rabbit. Waiter Elmer Fudd is at a loss where he'll get fresh rabbit at that time of night until he finds Bugs Bunny feasting on carrots. With time running out, Fudd tries to get Bugs into the pot.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs ends up in a land of ferocious apes. Mistaken for a baby, a female gorilla takes Bugs into her care. The male gorilla, however, hates having kids.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Papa Bear decides it's time for the three bears to hibernate in order to have a good winter's nap. Unfortunately, everything works against him.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky Pig fights to get a room in the only vacant hotel in town. Unfortunately, he must share his room with Daffy Duck, who irritates Porky and makes the night uncomfortable for him.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A weary Elmer Fudd retires to bed with hopes for a good night's sleep, but his slumber is disrupted by the incessant singing of Sylvester Cat, who is perched atop a fence beneath Elmer's window. Fudd resorts to increasingly violent methods to try to silence the pesky feline.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: After Bugs Bunny criticizes a championship boxer for beating up a boxer much smaller than him, the champion decides to challenge Bugs to a fight.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A baby kangaroo hops out of his zoo cage and roams into the surrounding city.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Mr. Buzzcut sends Beavis & Butt-head to the school nurse because they stink. They dance with her and get a massage.
videos:
Frank Sinatra with Bono - "I've Got You Under My Skin"
Doug E. Fresh - "I-ight (Alright)"
Melvins - "Hootch"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head try to kill a fly, and almost kill each other.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head try to earn workman's compensation...the hard way.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis is bitten by a dog; Butt-head takes him to the hospital, where a psychotic doctor gives him unnecessary shots for treatment.
videos:
Missing Persons - "Words"
Faith No More - "Easy"
Public Image LTD - "The Body"
Biohazard with Onyx - "Judgment Night"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Our heroes are forced to go on Captain Dick Jackman's talk show and get frustrated when they don't score with some girls named Lolita & Tanqueray.
"Woke Up With A Monster" (Cheap Trick)
"If That's Your Boyfriend" (Me'Shell Ndege'Ocello)
"Bad To The Bone" (George Thorogood)

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
After watching "that Letter-dude" on TV, Beavis and Butt-head do a similar show for their class project.
videos:
Grim Reaper - "See You In Hell"
Donny Osmond - "Sacred Emotion"
Greta - "Fathom"

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After they see the attention an angsty emo kid gets from the ladies, Beavis and Butt-Head pretend to be sad and end up in a psychiatric hospital.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: On a field trip, Beavis and Butt-Head fall asleep in a school bus and wake up -- in 1832? A colonial village sends the boys through a time loop. Will they keep Van Driessen from being born in the past, or just ruin his life in the present?

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head fall into a sewer and think they have died and gone to hell.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis gets his arm trapped in a blood pressure testing machine in the supermarket, while Butt-head procrastinates rescuing him.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys must give oral reports on U.S. history, or face flunking ninth grade; Beavis tells about last week's dinner at Burger World; Butt-head reports on the time he kicked Beavis in the 'nads.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: While her house is being painted, Miss Yvonne accepts an invitation to stay at the Playhouse. Perfumey as she is, Miss Yvonne is a stinker of a houseguest! Since Pee-wee's too "nice" to ask her to leave, he has to get a little help from Jambi to get her house painted lickety split. Now, the Cowntess wants to stay over while her barn is painted. What will Pee-wee do?

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: The laughs and fun never "stop" when Pee-wee, Miss Yvonne, and the Playhouse crew pretend to take a plane trip around the world. Reba has to steal Miss Yvonne, who's the plane's stewardess, away for some girl talk because she's got a date. With Pee-wee and Miss Yvonne acting as chaperones, Reba and her date take in a drive-in movie - Playhouse style!

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The girls get a cleaning woman to clean the apartment and the woman unearths an application Max has for pastry school. Caroline urges to go and apply but Max doesn't think she can get in. They go and Caroline finds herself attracted to the instructor Nicolas. He asks Max to make a tart as her project to see if she can get in. Max thinks it's a piece of cake till she finds out what a tart is.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The First Day Of School
programme.desc: Max attempts to make a good impression on her first day of pastry school, but finds the class clown to be extremely distracting – and intriguing. Meanwhile, Caroline dresses to impress Max’s handsome teacher, Nicolas.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline attends her former nanny’s funeral, thinking the family will be honored to have her, but quickly finds out they have no idea who she is.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan journey to the premiere of No Holds Barred to present an extraordinary lineup of in-ring competition, featuring a main event contest between Hillbilly Jim and Greg "The Hammer" Valentine. Plus, a look at an unusual pizza delivery man living an American dream, The Twin Towers in action, and more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: When Gene finds an expensive talking toilet out in the woods, he and his friends decide to protect it as much as they can while an evil toilet hunter is looking for the toilet.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert Barone finds himself in the most unlikely position of having to choose one of three women-girlfriend Amy, ex-wife Joanne, or Italian bombshell Stefania - to share the future with him. Unfortunately, when he tells Amy about his recent reunion with ex-wife however, it all starts to come apart.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray inadvertently commits petslaughter on Ally's hamster while Debra and the kids are away for the weekend. When Ray breaks the difficult news to Ally about Pumpernickel's untimely demise, Debra insists that they give the beloved pet a proper burial, complete with eulogies, but Ray thinks that it's ridicules to morn a pet.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug Heffernan (played by Kevin James) finds Deacon Palmer (played by Victor Williams) out with another woman, Angie (played by Patricia McBride) and Deacon makes him promise not to say anything. When Carrie (played by Leah Remini) finds out, she begins to question their marriage because Doug and her apparently have different views on whether or not it is cheating.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After Deacon and Kelly's separation, Carrie begins to wonder about her own marriage. She and Doug agree to put their sex lives on hold for a few weeks because Carrie feels that they should resolve arguments verbally, not always physically. Meanwhile, Arthur dates a woman who claims she was once Frank Sinatra's girlfriend.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George gets Steinbrenner hooked on eggplant calzones from a local Italian restaurant. But George gets banned from the restaurant when he's accused of stealing from the tip jar. He then asks Kramer to pick up the calzones, but Kramer gets kicked out when he tries to pay with pennies.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House finds that some of his own fellowship students will do whatever it takes, when they deal with a woman who believes she can talk to the dead.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Fearing that he only has three more years to live, Homer takes the family on holiday to Florida where,after Homer has made a fool of himself at a rock concert,he accidentally runs over Captain Jack,the famous long-living alligator. The family flee but are eventually captured though when it transpires that the alligator is not dead,as feared,they are released on the condition they never return to Florida.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Inspired by a tango film, Lisa signs up for dance lessons but the teacher, ex-child star Vicki Valentine, specializes in tap, at which Ralph is surprisingly good but Lisa hopeless. Professor Frink gives her a pair of self-dancing shoes but they go out of control and Lisa has to be tripped up by Homer to disable them. Bart and Milhouse, meanwhile, go camping in the shopping mall, thwarting Wiggum's attempts to flush them out with a puma.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: It's a Mad, Mad, Mad, Mad Marge
programme.desc: Otto's girlfriend seems to be usurping Marge's role when she moves in with the family after a dispute over heavy metal.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A parody documentary in the style of the show 'Behind the Music' uses clips from previous episodes to show how the Simpsons first became famous and the problems that the fame brought them, culminating in an acrimonious break-up. However they all get back together again to appear in a supposed awards show hosted by Willie Nelson but specifically designed to engineer their reunion.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: This year's Halloween story sees The Simpsons as "The Munsters" (with everyone except Lisa getting killed), Homer as a wandering spirit on the hunt for a good deed in "G-G-Ghost D-D-Dad," Bart and Lisa as peasants in a fairy tale forest in "Scary Tales Can Come True," and Lisa inadvertently dooming mankind by rescuing a dolphin in "Night of the Dolphin."

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While calling Animal Control over a badger taking residence in Santa's Little Helper's doghouse, Homer discovers that Springfield has two different area codes–and ends up leading a revolt that splits the town in two.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty takes parenting lessons from Homer when, during an outdoor book fair, a girl tells Krusty that she is his long-lost daughter from a one-night stand with a female soldier who fought during the first Gulf War.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa falls for the teenaged leader of a militant environmentalist group and tries to impress him by living in Springfield's oldest tree in order to keep it from being cut down.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer asks Burns for a raise and is told he can have one providing he becomes a 'prank monkey', playing stupid jokes on people and generally humiliating himself. He does,however,get the raise and decides to atone for the pranks by throwing toys which he has bought for the children of Springfield into the crowd from a carnival float. Burns tries to get in on the act by throwing fish guts.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer decides to buy a computer after turning up to the Plant to find it closed by e-mail. He then creates his own website and it eventually gains attention when he posts gossip. Homer dubs himself as "Mr. X" to conceal his identity. When he reveals that he is Mr. X, he becomes drugged into a mysterious island where people who know too much are imprisoned after Homer starts writing conspiracy theories such as flu shots.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: While celebrate firing "Stone Cold" Steve Austin, Mr. McMahon becomes the hunted as the Rattlesnake looks to strike back at his former employer. Kane looks to finally lay The Undertaker to rest in a Casket Match. Also in action, Ken Shamrock battles X-Pac, The Rock takes on D-Lo Brown, and much, much more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny puts the motel in jeopardy when he rents rooms to a dubious enterprise and David helps Moira with a film audition.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Stevie visit a nearby golf course to pitch a package deal with the motel, but Johnny becomes distracted by his desire to play a round of golf. After agreeing to attend Twyla's murder mystery party, Moira must help her recruit other guests. Alexis entertains a suggestion from Ted to go back to school.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline put their apartment up for rent on Airbnb, claiming it offers an authentic "Williamsburg experience," a group of supermodels decide it would be a fun place for a girls' weekend.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get a bank loan so they can mass-produce their cupcake T-shirts to sell at high-end boutiques. Also, the diner gang poses in Chestnut’s barn as part of a holiday decorating contest Sophie entered.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline select a factory to produce their cupcake T-shirts, but become suspicious of the work conditions there due to their overly-enthusiastic employees.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Past And The Furious
programme.desc: Caroline is shocked when she receives a Lamborghini for her birthday, which her father ordered for her four years ago. Meanwhile, Oleg has something big planned for Sophie.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max, Caroline, Earl and Han help Oleg move into Sophie’s apartment until she begins turning away all of his things.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are arrested for breaking into a cute guy’s apartment to recover the rings Caroline left behind after she spent the night.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get into a legal battle with two prep school teens who knocked off their cupcake T-shirts and are selling them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline find out that one of their customers at the diner is actually a successful photographer who has been keeping a secret.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Sophie’s wedding shower is interrupted by a hostage situation and Max and Caroline’s cupcake business gets unwanted attention when a tenant is arrested while wearing their company’s T-shirt.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A handsome businessman returns Max and Caroline’s missing cat, Nancy, who comes home with a big surprise.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to bring in extra money to help pay off their bank loan by taking second jobs as a pastry chef and a hostess at a chic restaurant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max pursues Nashit, a handsome new waiter at The High, despite Joe’s “no hook-up” rule.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie takes Max and Caroline shopping for their bridesmaids’ dresses, the girls enlist the diner gang’s help to hide the ugliest ones, in the hopes of avoiding complete and utter humiliation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Look Of The Irish
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to break Nash into the modeling business in an effort to bring in extra money acting as his managers. Also, Sophie and Oleg attempt to keep celibate for 30 days before their wedding.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline’s attempt to break Nash into the modeling business comes to a screeching halt when his mother arrives from Ireland to retrieve her son.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: AFC Richmond play their final game of the season.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: American football coach Ted Lasso is hired by a wealthy divorcée to coach the English soccer team AFC Richmond.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony juggles Carmela's stalled spec house, Janice and Bobby's ambitions, and pressure from Johnny Sack's legal troubles, while Vito's new small-town life tests how far he can really run from his old one.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Phil takes out his revenge on Vito, while Tony grows frustrated with Anthony's lack of ambition or work ethic.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: With time running out to find answers, Chidi must sort through his life -- and afterlives -- before he can provide the help everyone needs.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: The group scrambles to devise a new afterlife system that the Good and Bad Places can agree on before it's too late. Chidi has a newfound confidence.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: While the humans search for test candidates, Michael trains a group of architects. Eleanor doesn't want Chidi to learn too much about her past.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Daemon and the Sea Snake battle the Crabfeeder. The realm celebrates Aegon's second nameday. Rhaenyra faces the prospect of marriage.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Robb and Catelyn arrive at Riverrun for Lord Hoster Tully's funeral. Tywin names Tyrion the new Master of Coin. Arya says goodbye to Hot Pie. The Night's Watch returns to Craster's. Brienne and Jaime are taken prisoner.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: And Now His Watch Is Ended
programme.desc: The Night's Watch takes stock. Varys meets his better. Arya is taken to the commander of the Brotherhood. Dany exchanges a chain for a whip.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A victim of a mugging presents with neurological symptoms and begins to mirror the behaviors of his doctors. Foreman joins the new fellows in seeking a diagnosis; Cameron and Chase takes bets on whom House will fire next.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy are about to take that last long walk at the dog pound when a sweet rich old lady falls for them and adopts them.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In this parody of Pinocchio, Renwaldo, the village of Egg Smithëe, makes a puppet boy name Egg Yölkeo.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren can't stand Stimpy anymore, so he sends Stimpy on a one-way ticket out of town. Then they are hit by a bus and get united (literally) in a cheap hospital.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren hits Stimpy in the nose and he starts to sing with a crooner-like voice. Stimpy becames famous, until his nose leaves him because of being slapped many times.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy are going to be the first dog and cat sent up to space in the Russian space program

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: No license, no money, no problem! Beavis and Butt-Head shanghai a used car salesman on a joyride that leaves the car in pieces. Will they walk away from this one, or is everything wrecked for good?

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: On a field trip, Beavis and Butt-Head fall asleep in a school bus and wake up -- in 1832? A colonial village sends the boys through a time loop. Will they keep Van Driessen from being born in the past, or just ruin his life in the present?

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Seismic Zonkers (Craig & Jana) vs. Brain Teasers (Kevin & Siema)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Wannabees (Jermaine & Kim) vs. Perfect Strangers (Chris & Melissa)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Laverne and Shirley Kill Each Other will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
You can't put a price tag on friendship. Well, in fact, you can, as Christine sadly discovers when her mother's monthly friendship checks to the cast fail to arrive, leaving Christine friendless.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Twilight Zone Gets Redeveloped will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The crew fears a ghost is haunting the studio when strange things start happening. Lisa decides to perform an exorcism to get rid of it. In the sketches, the cast pokes fun at ESP and astrology, as well as magic in sketches like Genie of the Electric Lamp.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Hobby Days will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine talks about fashion, her latest hobby.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Six Wives of Henry VIII will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast parodies historical events, as Alasdair traces his family history and Christine writes a research essay.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Smurfs Meet Godzilla will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast must be brave to overcome stage fright as the show's ratings are higher then ever.
The kids discuss courage. Kevin tests his courage by wearing a suit of armor.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Real People's Armpits will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The show's cast competes to see who has the perfect body.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Miss Piggy Sold to a Sausage Factory will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
To raise money to keep the show on the air, the cast tries to sell water and green slime to viewers.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Around the World in 80 Days will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross appears interested in world travel and foreign cultures, like the rest of the cast. Actually, Ross is only interested in playing "exchange bank."

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Masterpiece Theater will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine tries to become one of the shows writers, while Justin tries to track down the great author A. Nonymous. In the sketches, the cast looks at the classics of literature.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Hollywood's Jilted Wives vs. Cheating Husbands
programme.desc:
The Brads attempt to get the upper hand on the Jennifers in an all-star edition of MXC to kickoff 2006.
Games include: Rotating Surfboard of Death; Pole Riders; Hand Job; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Big Bird takes on big bets when MXC brings together two of the most polar opposites in America: The kiddie playground of Sesame Street and the adult playground known as Las Vegas.
Games include: Cruelette; Wall Buggers; Big Bird Droppings; Sinkers and Floaters

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Howard Stern's radio regulars go toe to toe against Hollywood rehab addicts and alkies...it's the classic battle of the wack pack vs. the crack pack. Games - Rotating Surfboards of Death, The Crate Escape, Sperm Whalers, and Sinkers and Floaters.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's 'Roid Rage' as the pumped up blood-doping Pro Athletes take on the Fashion industry's Super Models...it's the classic battle of Anabolic Steroids versus Anorexic mind voids! Games - Eat Shitake, Spit Ball, Irritable Bowl Syndrome, and Log Drop.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Justice vs. TV Motor Sports Shows
programme.desc: It's Hollywood infamy and mechanical reverie as Celebrity Justice takes on TV's Motor shows. It's the classic battle of Fallen Stars and Souped-up Cars. Games - Sack Lunch, Wall Buggers, Little Man in the Boat, and Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's a cinematic bonanza as the big screen Tough Guys go head-to-head with the box office Chick Flicks...it's the classic battle of Rambo versus Romance. Games - Slippery Slope of Slanted Death, Tumbling Dominoes of Doom, Catch Hell, and Log Drop.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: The World of Hip Hop vs. Hollywood Horror Movies
programme.desc: It's music and monsters as the world of Hip Hop takes on Hollywood Horror Movies. It's the classic battle of Rappers vs. Rippers. Games - Cheeze Squeezers, Runaway Stump, Dash to Death, and Sinkers and Floaters.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Mega-Millionaires vs. Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: It's have-mores versus has-beens as the mega-millionaires take on the Where are they Nows? It's the classic battle of Gazillions vs. Gilligans. Games - Great Holes of Glory, Mud Butlers, Ball Busters, and Rotating Surfboards of Death.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: All the prodigal Lanes come home at once, forcing Jane to temporarily move in with Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jake has a heart attack and his mother comes to the Morgendorffer household to take care of him; his heart attack inspires Quinn to study to become a heart doctor; two radio DJs broadcast their show outside Lawndale High.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Quinn have to take a road trip to bail Jane and Trent out of a traffic violation.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Secret service agents visit Lawndale High, and urge students to keep an eye of for students that might be different, and thus a threaten their safety, as well as the safety of the country. Though Daria & Jane become suspect numbers one and two, they actually start to notice an outbreak of weirdness among their fellow students, faculty members, and even relatives.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The whole school goes on a mandatory-voluntary harbor cruise to raise money via casino gambling.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jane starts dating a guy, to the annoyance of her misanthropic friend Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: For an economics project, Daria and Jodie team up to start a company.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: While on a hill trek, Daria's class gets caught in an unexpected snowstorm.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Four Tasks of Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback kidnaps Penfold to get Danger Mouse to bring him four ingredients needed for an evil spell. Danger Mouse meets Count Duckula when one ingredient turns out to be the feather of a vampire duck.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback shrinks himself and enters Colonel K's body to learn all of the Colonel's secrets.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback vows to blow up every single signpost in every country unless he is proclaimed emperor of the world. DM and Penfold set out for the Toad's secret island hideout somewhere in the pacific ocean.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback is determined to steal plans that Danger Mouse and Penfold are transporting from Australia to London.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Strange Case of the Ghost Bus
programme.desc: Nine Top Secret cargo carrying ships have disappeared after encountering a ghost bus. DM finds the Ghost bus depot on a small island in the Indian Ocean where Baron Greenback is producing buses made of ice.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: After half the world's most famous monuments have vanished, Dangermouse and Penfold head for America, where they find the Tower of London in the old west. It soon becomes apparent that Baron Greenback has invented a machine to shrink, transport and de-shrink objects (and heroes like Dangermouse).

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Having just missed catching Baron Greenback in Tokyo, Dangermouse gets hold of the villain's digital hideout locater (disguised as a book). Using this device, DM and Penfold take a tour of the Baron's various hideouts hidden all around the world.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman talks about horses and reveals the most disgusting animal in the world before describing just how does a refrigerator stays cold.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman shows the identifying structure of human fingerprints, and explains why humans fart, what this gaseous emission is made of and why it smells...

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Wacky scientist Beakman and his assistant Josie, and Lester, an actor dressed as a mangy rat, answers an inquiry about where puddles go after it has rained. Although Lester suggests they are licked up by stray dogs, Beakman proceeds to explain the mysteries of rain and evaporation using a variety of household props. In the "Beak-Mania" portion of the show, Beakman attempts to answer, in rapid succession, short viewer inquiries on topics such as living prehistoric animals (the platypus), the most commonly used word in the English language and the heaviest animal in the history of the world (blue whale). Then, after a trick in which he balances a pair of forks atop a toothpick, Beakman forces Josie to reveal the question for the show's final segment, "What is snot?" Turning to volcanoes, Beakman explains the processes in the Earth's interior that create, and then discharge, molten magma. With the aid of film footage of volcanic eruptions, Beakman employs a foam rubber model of the Earth and a rubber glove to show how magma moves from deep inside the Earth and appears on the surface as lava. Finally, asked by Josie about why some volcanoes explode, Beakman uses a cherry pie to demonstrate the power, and mess, of a volcanic eruption.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains the laws of gravity; Galileo shows the effects of gravity;. Sir Isaac Newton explains inertia; how a seat belt helps save lives.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.sub-title: Noises at Night, Beakmania & The Nose
programme.desc: Beakman explains why your house creaks at night. And then explains the purpose of snot and mucus in your nose.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: More than 22,000 different species of fish live in the oceans, lakes, and rivers of the world. Fish come in all shapes and sizes. Some eat water plants, some eat other fish. Lampreys and some jawless fish suck onto other fish for food. Stone fish live on ocean bottoms and camouflage themselves as rocks. Puffer fish blow themselves up like a balloon, only they’re covered with spines. There are tons of strange and cool-looking fish everywhere.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Transportation is all about moving people and their things. You can transport yourself around using your feet, a bike, a car, a train – anything that gets you going from one place to another. Humans have been moving around from place to place ever since they’ve been on Earth. That’s how we get our food and our materials like lumber, concrete and computers from one place to another.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When the Wattersons arrive at the mall for a day of shopping, they struggle to find a space in the parking lot. They search for a space everywhere -- using all their intelligence, cunning and trickery -- but as the day goes on, the desperate hunt for a space becomes more important than the trip to the mall itself.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard's trip to the store to find mayonnaise for the family's burger night turns into a Neverending Story-meets-Conan the Barbarian-style quest where he squares off against Tina Rex's father, Mr. Rex.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin see an advert for the latest, cutting-edge Bobert operating system and excitedly talk Bobert into downloading it. At first it seems like a massive improvement, but the many flaws in the new operating system become increasingly apparent.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Sarah creates a comic of Gumball as a superhero named LaserHeart, prompting Gumball to act like one in real life.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Fearing that she may be sick of him due to a missing emoji on her message to him, Gumball sends Penny on a romantic quest to rekindle their love, which turns into a disaster when Penny gets lost in the woods.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball goes onto Elmore Stream-It, but is immediately interrupted by Darwin's protest. Gumball begs Darwin to just let him see one video, but Darwin continues. He gets Gumball to back away from the computer and turn around, revealing that he has the mouse in his hand.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball sets out to prove to Penny's father that he's good enough for his daughter. Gumball decides that the best way to prove to Mr. Fitzgerald that he's good enough for Penny is to become his apprentice for the day. The problem is, Mr. Fitzgerald has an important interview and Gumball's enthusiastic attempts to help him might actually do more harm than good, both to Mr. Fitzgerald's business and to Gumball's future with Penny.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: To prove to Darwin that he can be unpredictable, Gumball decides to hug the first person he sees -- an anthropomorphic hot dog -- and Gumball ends up in over his head when he and the Hot Dog Guy can't stop bumping into each other.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: After being framed for feeding coffee- and prune juice-laced nuts to the squirrels, Darwin tries to prove to Gumball that Mr. Robinson's wife, Margaret, is a good person underneath her destructive, sociopathic tendencies, but when Darwin sees just how evil Margaret is, the duo decide to catch her committing grand theft auto.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Alan allegedly ditches Gumball's dinner date with him, Gumball is desperate to track him down and make him pay for his betrayal, only to learn that Alan is taking his mother, Jessica, to the hospital for an organ transplant.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin, Beastboy and Cyborg decide to have a crazy Boys' Night Out without Starfire. Starfire brings Raven along to break Jinx out of jail to have a Girl's Night Out ; Beast Boy's indecisiveness causes him to be fired from the Teen Titans and the Teen Titans have to find a replacement Titan member, ranging from Vixen, B'wana Beast, Detective Chimp, and the Wonder Twins.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy's behavior causes him to be fired from the Teen Titans and replaced by a pair of shape-shifting twins.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Envious of the other Titans' superpowers following their fight with the H.I.V.E. Five (consisting of Mammoth, Gizmo, Jinx, Billy Numerous, and See-More), Robin wants some of his own. After a disastrous scientific experiment goes awry that merges him with the DNA of the American Robin, he convinces Raven to use her magic to give him superpowers even though she claims that superpowers are a curse.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg plugs into the control panel of the Titans tower and drives the others crazy.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Starfire's new pet is a horribly dangerous parasite, but Robin is the only one who is suspicious of the beast.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Starfire is the only Titan invited to the Titans East Annual Dance Party, Beast Boy teaches her how to lie so that she can spare the other Titans' feelings - but her lies turn all of the Titans against each other.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: While trying to entice Raven into joining a meatball party, Cyborg fires a half-meat, half-robot mystery meatball into her mouth, cracking her tooth and releasing the demon octopus that lives in her mouth.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Robin's long bo-staff is accidentally broken by the other four Titans, he seeks a new one at the mystical Tree of the Universe.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy thinks the villainess Terra is his new girlfriend, but she is just using him to gain access to all of the Titans' secrets. Only Raven suspects Terra, but the others just think that Raven is jealous.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans confidently expect to win an upcoming dodgeball championship, even though the only thing they ever practice is their victory dance. Then they come up against an unbeatable dodge ball team - the H.I.V.E.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren is suffering from horrible allergies. Little does he know that he may be allergic to his best pal Stimpy.
